heading: Social security and tier 1 railroad retirement benefits
label: 86 Social security and tier 1 railroad retirement benefits
notes: Editorial Notes

References in TextThe Social Security Act, referred to in subsecs. (a)(1) and (d)(1)(A), (3), (4)(A), (5), is , , which is classified generally to chapter 7 (§ 301 et seq.) of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. Title II of the Act is classified generally to subchapter II (§ 401 et seq.) of Title 42. Sections 207, 224, and 708 of the Act are classified to sections 407, 424a, and 909 of Title 42, respectively. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see and Tables.act Aug. 14, 1935, ch. 53149 Stat. 620section 1305 of Title 42The Railroad Retirement Act of 1974, referred to in subsec. (d)(3), (4), is , as amended generally by , , , which is classified generally to subchapter IV (§ 231 et seq.) of chapter 9 of Title 45, Railroads. Section 3(a)(1), (f)(3) of the Act is classified to section 231b(a)(1), (f)(3) of Title 45. For further details and complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Codification note set out preceding , , and Tables.act Aug. 29, 1935, ch. 812Pub. children.label: A
children.text: one-half of the social security benefits received during the taxable year, or

children.label: B
children.text: one-half of the excess described in subsection (b)(1).

children.label: i
children.text: 85 percent of such excess, plus

children.label: ii
children.text: the lesser of the amount determined under paragraph (1) or an amount equal to one-half of the difference between the adjusted base amount and the base amount of the taxpayer, or

children.label: B
children.text: 85 percent of the social security benefits received during the taxable year.

children.heading: Taxpayers to whom subsection (a) applies
children.label: b Taxpayers to whom subsection (a) applies
i
children.text: the modified adjusted gross income of the taxpayer for the taxable year, plus

children.label: ii
children.text: one-half of the social security benefits received during the taxable year, exceeds

children.label: B
children.text: the base amount.

children.label: A
children.text: determined without regard to this section and sections 85(c), 135, 137, 221, 911, 931, and 933, and

children.label: B
children.text: increased by the amount of interest received or accrued by the taxpayer during the taxable year which is exempt from tax.

children.heading: Base amount and adjusted base amount
children.label: c Base amount and adjusted base amount
A
children.text: except as otherwise provided in this paragraph, $25,000,

children.label: B
children.text: $32,000 in the case of a joint return, and

children.label: i
children.text: is married as of the close of the taxable year (within the meaning of section 7703) but does not file a joint return for such year, and

children.label: ii
children.text: does not live apart from his spouse at all times during the taxable year.

children.label: A
children.text: except as otherwise provided in this paragraph, $34,000,

children.label: B
children.text: $44,000 in the case of a joint return, and

children.label: C
children.text: zero in the case of a taxpayer described in paragraph (1)(C).

children.label: A
children.text: a monthly benefit under title II of the Social Security Act, or

children.label: B
children.text: a tier 1 railroad retirement benefit.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of this section, the amount of social security benefits received during any taxable year shall be reduced by any repayment made by the taxpayer during the taxable year of a social security benefit previously received by the taxpayer (whether or not such benefit was received during the taxable year).

children.heading: Denial of deduction
children.label: B Denial of deduction
children.text: If (but for this subparagraph) any portion of the repayments referred to in subparagraph (A) would have been allowable as a deduction for the taxable year under section 165, such portion shall be allowable as a deduction only to the extent it exceeds the social security benefits received by the taxpayer during the taxable year (and not repaid during such taxable year).

children.heading: Workmen’s compensation benefits substituted for social security benefits
children.label: 3 Workmen’s compensation benefits substituted for social security benefits
children.text: For purposes of this section, if, by reason of section 224 of the Social Security Act (or by reason of section 3(a)(1) of the Railroad Retirement Act of 1974), any social security benefit is reduced by reason of the receipt of a benefit under a workmen’s compensation act, the term “social security benefit” includes that portion of such benefit received under the workmen’s compensation act which equals such reduction.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of the annuity under the Railroad Retirement Act of 1974 equal to the amount of the benefit to which the taxpayer would have been entitled under the Social Security Act if all of the service after , of the employee (on whose employment record the annuity is being paid) had been included in the term “employment” as defined in the Social Security Act, andDecember 31, 1936

children.label: B
children.text: a monthly annuity amount under section 3(f)(3) of the Railroad Retirement Act of 1974.

children.heading: Effect of early delivery of benefit checks
children.label: 5 Effect of early delivery of benefit checks
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a), in any case where section 708 of the Social Security Act causes social security benefit checks to be delivered before the end of the calendar month for which they are issued, the benefits involved shall be deemed to have been received in the succeeding calendar month.

children.heading: Limitation on amount included where taxpayer receives lump-sum payment
children.label: e Limitation on amount included where taxpayer receives lump-sum payment
A
children.text: any portion of a lump-sum payment of social security benefits received during the taxable year is attributable to prior taxable years, and

children.label: B
children.text: the taxpayer makes an election under this subsection for the taxable year,

children.heading: Year to which benefit attributable
children.label: A Year to which benefit attributable
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, a social security benefit is attributable to a taxable year if the generally applicable payment date for such benefit occurred during such taxable year.

children.heading: Election
children.label: B Election
children.text: An election under this subsection shall be made at such time and in such manner as the Secretary shall by regulations prescribe. Such election, once made, may be revoked only with the consent of the Secretary.

children.heading: Treatment as pension or annuity for certain purposes
children.label: f Treatment as pension or annuity for certain purposes

children.text: section 22(c)(3)(A) (relating to reduction for amounts received as pension or annuity),
section 32(c)(2) (defining earned income),
section 219(f)(1) (defining compensation), and
section 911(b)(1) (defining foreign earned income),
